cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about the World Bank study from 1974 mentioned in the text. The user is asking what the study found regarding economic growth in backward (developing) countries. Looking back at the passage: "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." Wait, the wording here is a bit tricky. The study said that the growth had been of little benefit to a third of their population. So, if the benefit was only to a third, that implies that two-thirds didn't get much benefit. But
